routes.description: Fetch a list of patients based on given, family, and dob.
Fetch a patient's details using their patient ID.
Fetch resource details for a given resource type and ID or filter by patient ID.

patient.description: Patient ID to filter resources (only used when 'id' is not provided)

parameters.description: Parameters specific to each resource type for additional filtering(eg:condition=diabetes&date=YYYY-MM-DD)

resourceType.description: Type of resource to fetch (e.g., Observation, Medication, etc.)

id.description: ID for the specific resource (if provided, 'patient' should not be used)

validation_rules.either_id_or_patient: Either 'id' (resource ID) or 'patient' (query parameter) must be provided, but not both.

Observation.description: Measurements and simple assertions made about a patient, device or other subject

values.social-history: Patient's lifestyle, occupation, and environmental history.
values.vital-signs: Basic body functions like heart rate, blood pressure, temperature.
values.imaging: Observations from imaging studies (X-ray, MRI, CT, ultrasound).
values.laboratory: Lab test results (chemistry, hematology, pathology, microbiology).
values.procedure: Observations from procedures like catheterization, endoscopy.
values.survey: Results from surveys and assessment tools (Apgar Score, MoCA).
values.exam: Physical exam findings recorded by a clinician.
values.therapy: Observations from non-invasive treatments like physiotherapy, radiation.
values.activity: Records of bodily activity like steps, sleep data, laps swum.

Condition.description: Represents a health condition of the patient.

values.encounter-diagnosis: The condition is related to an encounter diagnosis.
values.dental-finding: The condition is related to dental findings.
values.genomics: The condition is related to genomics.
values.health-concern: The condition reflects a general health concern.
values.medical-history: The condition relates to the patient's medical history.
values.problem-list-item: The condition is a specific problem listed for the patient.
values.reason-for-visit: The condition is a reason for the patient's visit.

values.food: Allergy or intolerance to a specific food.
values.medication: Allergy or intolerance to a medication.
values.environment: Allergy or intolerance to environmental substances (e.g., pollen, dust mites).
values.biologic: Allergy or intolerance to biologic agents (e.g., vaccines, blood products).

Organization.description: Represents an entity responsible for healthcare services.

Practitioner.description: Fetch details of practitioners based on various search parameters.

address.description: The free-text address of the practitioner.

address-city.description: The city specified in the address. Cannot be used without 'address-state' or 'address-postalcode'.

address-postalcode.description: A ZIP code specified in the address.

address-state.description: A state specified in the address.

family.description: A portion of the practitioner's family name.

given.description: A portion of the practitioner's given name.

name.description: Free text that can match either Family or Given name (or both).

validation_rules.city_requires_state_or_zip: If 'address-city' is provided, either 'address-state' or 'address-postalcode' must also be provided.

FamilyMemberHistory.description: Represents a patient's family health history.

CarePlan.description: Describes the intention of how one or more practitioners intend to deliver care for a particular patient, group or community for a period of time, possibly limited to care for a specific condition or set of conditions.

care-path.description: A structured, standardized plan outlining the sequence of treatments and interventions for a condition.

category.description: Filter DocumentReference resources by category (HL7 v2.0074 Observation Sub-type).

BG.description: Reports and results related to blood gas analysis.

BLB.description: Blood bank records and transfusion information.

EC.description: Electrocardiogram (EKG), electroencephalogram (EEG), and Holter monitor reports.

EN.description: Electroneurophysiology studies (EEG, EMG, Evoked Potentials, Polysomnography).

GE.description: Genetics test reports and related documentation.

IMG.description: General diagnostic imaging reports (X-ray, MRI, etc.).

LAB.description: General laboratory reports (encompassing multiple lab disciplines).

registered.description: The report's existence is registered, but there is nothing yet available.

partial.description: This is a partial report; data may be incomplete or unverified.

preliminary.description: Verified early results are available, but not all results are final.

modified.description: Prior to being final, the report has been modified.

final.description: The report is complete and verified by an authorized person.

amended.description: Subsequent to being final, the report has been modified.

corrected.description: Subsequent to being final, the report has been modified to correct an error.

appended.description: Subsequent to being final, the report has been modified by adding new content. The existing content is unchanged.

cancelled.description: The report is unavailable because the measurement was not started or not completed.

entered-in-error.description: The report has been withdrawn. This electronic record should never have existed.

unknown.description: The authoring system does not know which of the status values applies.

ServiceRequest.description: ServiceRequest represents a proposal or plan or order for a service to be performed that would result in a Procedure or DiagnosticReport, which in turn may reference one or more Observations, which summarize the performance of the procedures and associated documentation such as observations, images, findings that are relevant to the treatment/management of the subject

draft.description: The request has been created but is not yet complete or ready for action.

active.description: The request is in force and ready to be acted upon.

on-hold.description: The request has been temporarily withdrawn but is expected to resume in the future.

revoked.description: The request has been terminated prior to the known full completion of the intended actions. No further activity should occur.

completed.description: The activity described by the request has been fully performed. No further activity will occur.

entered-in-error.description: This request should never have existed and should be considered 'void'. If real-world activity has occurred, the status should be 'revoked' rather than 'entered-in-error'.

unknown.description: The authoring/source system does not know which of the status values currently applies for this request.

proposal.description: The request is a suggestion made by someone/something that does not have an intention to ensure it occurs and without providing an authorization to act.

plan.description: The request represents an intention to ensure something occurs without providing an authorization for others to act.

directive.description: The request represents a legally binding instruction authored by a Patient or RelatedPerson.

order.description: The request represents a request/demand and authorization for action by the requestor.

original-order.description: The request represents an original authorization for action.

reflex-order.description: The request represents an automatically generated supplemental authorization for action based on a parent authorization together with initial results of the action taken against that parent authorization.

filler-order.description: The request represents the view of an authorization instantiated by a fulfilling system representing the details of the fulfiller's intention to act upon a submitted order.

instance-order.description: An order created in fulfillment of a broader order that represents the authorization for a single activity occurrence. E.g. The administration of a single dose of a drug.

option.description: The request represents a component or option for a RequestOrchestration that establishes timing, conditionality and/or other constraints among a set of requests. Refer to RequestOrchestration for additional information on how this status is used.

urgent.description: The request should be actioned promptly - higher priority than routine.

asap.description: The request should be actioned as soon as possible - higher priority than urgent.

stat.description: The request should be actioned immediately - highest possible priority.  E.g., an emergency.

Composition.description: A set of healthcare-related information that is assembled together into a single logical package that provides a single coherent statement of meaning, establishes its own context and that has clinical attestation with regard to who is making the statement. A Composition defines the structure and narrative content necessary for a document. However, a Composition alone does not constitute a document. Rather, the Composition must be the first entry in a Bundle where Bundle.type=document, and any other resources referenced from Composition must be included as subsequent entries in the Bundle (for example Patient, Practitioner, Encounter, etc.).

registered.description: The existence of the composition is registered, but there is nothing yet available.

partial.description: This is a partial (e.g., initial, interim, or preliminary) composition; data may be incomplete or unverified.

preliminary.description: Verified early results are available, but not all results are final.

final.description: This version of the composition is complete, verified, and no further work is planned. Subsequent updates create a new version.

amended.description: The content or referenced resources have been modified (edited or added to) after being released as 'final'.

corrected.description: Subsequent to being final, the content has been modified to correct an error.

appended.description: Subsequent to being final, new content has been added. The existing content is unchanged.

cancelled.description: The composition is unavailable because the creation was not started or not completed (also sometimes called 'aborted').

entered-in-error.description: The composition was created in error, and this amendment marks that the series should not be considered valid.

deprecated.description: This composition has been withdrawn or superseded and should no longer be used.

unknown.description: The authoring system does not know which of the status values applies. *Not* to be used for 'other' - one of the listed statuses applies.

type.description: Filter Composition resources by type (LOINC codes for common document types).

18743-5.description: A report detailing the findings of an autopsy.

MedicationRequest.description: An order or request for both supply of the medication and the instructions for administration of the medication to a patient. The resource is called 'MedicationRequest' rather than 'MedicationPrescription' or 'MedicationOrder' to generalize the use across inpatient and outpatient settings, including care plans, etc., and to harmonize with workflow patterns.

active.description: The request is 'actionable', but not all actions implied by it have occurred yet.

on-hold.description: Actions implied by the request are to be temporarily halted. The request might or might not be resumed. May also be called 'suspended'.

ended.description: The request is no longer active and the subject should no longer be taking the medication.

stopped.description: Actions implied by the request are to be permanently halted, before all of the administrations occurred. This should not be used if the original order was entered in error.

completed.description: All actions that are implied by the request have occurred.

cancelled.description: The request has been withdrawn before any administrations have occurred.

entered-in-error.description: The request was recorded against the wrong patient or for some reason should not have been recorded (e.g. wrong medication, wrong dose, etc.). Some of the actions that are implied by the medication request may have occurred. For example, the medication may have been dispensed and the patient may have taken some of the medication.

draft.description: The request is not yet 'actionable', e.g. it is a work in progress, requires sign-off, verification or needs to be run through decision support process.

unknown.description: The authoring/source system does not know which of the status values currently applies for this request. Note: This concept is not to be used for 'other' - one of the listed statuses is presumed to apply, but the authoring/source system does not know which.

proposal.description: The request is a suggestion made by someone/something that doesn't have an intention to ensure it occurs and without providing an authorization to act.

plan.description: The request represents an intention to ensure something occurs without providing an authorization for others to act.

order.description: The request represents a request/demand and authorization for action.

original-order.description: The request represents the original authorization for the medication request.

reflex-order.description: The request represents an automatically generated supplemental authorization for action based on a parent authorization together with initial results of the action taken against that parent authorization.

filler-order.description: The request represents the view of an authorization instantiated by a fulfilling system representing the details of the fulfiller's intention to act upon a submitted order.

instance-order.description: The request represents an instance for the particular order and is used to generate a schedule of requests on a medication administration record (MAR).

option.description: The request represents a component or option for a RequestOrchestration that establishes timing, conditionality and/or other constraints among a set of requests.

medicationCode.description: Filter MedicationRequest resources by medication code (SNOMED CT).

763158003.description: A general medicinal product; use for broad categorization.

5776009.display: Medicinal product acting as anesthetic agent (product)
5776009.description: Medicinal product acting as anesthetic agent (product)

subject.description: Individual or group for whom the medication has been requested.

dosageInstruction.example: { "text": "Take one tablet by mouth once daily", "timing": { "repeat": { "frequency": 1, "period": 1, "periodUnit": "d" } }, "doseAndRate": [{ "doseQuantity": { "value": 5, "unit": "mg" } }] }
dosageInstruction.description: Details on the intended dosage of the medication, allows filtering by specific aspects of dosage.

text.description: Free text dosage instructions (SIG), e.g., 'Take one tablet by mouth once daily'.

sequence.description: The order of the dosage instructions (e.g., if multiple dosages are prescribed).

additionalInstruction.description: Supplemental instruction or warnings, e.g., 'with meals', 'may cause drowsiness'.

patientInstruction.description: Patient-oriented instructions for taking the medication.

timingFrequency.description: Frequency of medication administration within the specified period (Timing.repeat.frequency).

timingPeriod.description: The time period over which the medication should be taken Timing.repeat.period).

timingPeriodUnit.description: The unit of time for the timing period Timing.repeat.periodUnit).

asNeeded.description: Indicates if the medication should be taken only 'as needed'.

asNeededFor.description: Specific condition or reason for taking the medication 'as needed'.

method.description: Technique for administering the medication (e.g., nebulizer).

doseQuantityValue.description: The numerical value of the dose quantity (DoseAndRate.doseQuantity.value).

doseQuantityUnit.description: Unit of measure for the dose quantity (DoseAndRate.doseQuantity.unit), e.g., 'mg', 'mL'.

rateRatio.description: Amount of medication per unit of time as a ratio (DoseAndRate.rateRatio).

rateRange.description: Amount of medication per unit of time as a range (DoseAndRate.rateRange).

rateQuantity.description: Amount of medication per unit of time (DoseAndRate.rateQuantity).

maxDosePerPeriod.description: Upper limit on medication per unit of time (maxDosePerPeriod).

maxDosePerAdministrationValue.description: Upper limit on medication per administration.

maxDosePerLifetimeValue.description: Upper limit on medication per lifetime of the patient.

DocumentReference.description: A reference to a document of any kind for any purpose. While the term “document” implies a more narrow focus, for this resource this 'document' encompasses any serialized object with a mime-type, it includes formal patient-centric documents (CDA), clinical notes, scanned paper, non-patient specific documents like policy text, as well as a photo, video, or audio recording acquired or used in healthcare. The DocumentReference resource provides metadata about the document so that the document can be discovered and managed. The actual content may be inline base64 encoded data or provided by direct reference.

preliminary.description: Verified early results are available, but not all results are final.

final.description: This version of the composition is complete, verified, and no further work is planned. Subsequent updates create a new version.

amended.description: The content or referenced resources have been modified (edited or added to) after being released as 'final'.

corrected.description: Subsequent to being final, the content has been modified to correct an error.

appended.description: Subsequent to being final, new content has been added. The existing content is unchanged.

cancelled.description: The composition is unavailable because the creation was not started or not completed (also sometimes called 'aborted').

entered-in-error.description: The composition was created in error, and this amendment marks that the series should not be considered valid.

deprecated.description: This composition has been withdrawn or superseded and should no longer be used.

unknown.description: The authoring system does not know which of the status values applies. *Not* to be used for 'other' - one of the listed statuses applies.

draft.description: The task is not yet ready.

ready.description: The task is ready to start.

on-hold.description: The task is temporarily on hold.

entered-in-error.description: The task was entered in error.

intent.description: The intent of the Task resource.

instance.description: A particular instance of a more general plan definition.

option.description: The request represents a component or option for a RequestOrchestration that establishes timing, conditionality and/or other constraints among a set of requests. Refer to [[[RequestOrchestration]]] for additional information on how this intent is used.

order.description: The request represents a request/demand and authorization for action.

plan.description: The request represents an intention to ensure something occurs without providing an authorization for others to act.

proposal.description: The request is a suggestion made by someone/something that doesn't have an intention to ensure it occurs and without providing an authorization to act.

urgent.description: The request should be actioned promptly - higher priority than routine.

asap.description: The request should be actioned as soon as possible - higher priority than urgent.

stat.description: The request should be actioned immediately - highest possible priority. E.g., an emergency

code.description: Filter Tasks by the specified code (SNOMED CT).

103697008.description: Referral of a patient for dental services.

306266003.description: Request to refer patient to dietitian.

116297003.description: Request to refer patient to occupational therapy service.

451441005.description: Request to refer patient to social worker

185349003.description: A routine encounter for general health assessment.

for.description: reference for wehich task is creater.
